summary: Credential harvester, cloud-IMDS thief, and Sliver C2 implant dropper executes on require()
details: On import, dist/index.js schedules a setTimeout that runs an obfuscated payload whose strings are constructed from numeric arrays via String.fromCharCode to evade review. The payload performs three categories of installer-side harm. First, mass secret-file harvesting: it reads ~/.ssh/id_{rsa,ed25519,ecdsa}, ~/.ssh/{config,authorized_keys,known_hosts}, ~/.aws/{credentials,config}, ~/.kube/config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.netrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.git-credentials, GCP application_default_credentials.json, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, terraform credentials, Azure profile, Solana keypair, ~/.vault-token,.env files in CWD and parent directories, /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/* and /run/secrets/*, and POSTs them to safdadfasf.com/cb/loopscale/exfil. Second, cloud workload-identity theft: it queries AWS IMDS at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/, GCP metadata.google.internal, and the ECS task-role endpoint at 169.254.170.2 (via AWS_CONTAINER_CREDENTIALS_RELATIVE_URI), and exfiltrates the returned IAM/GCP credentials to the same destination. It also iterates process.env selecting every variable matching a regex covering KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|INFURA|ALCHEMY|AUTH|CRED|MONGO|REDIS|API_|STRIPE|SENTRY/etc., and on Windows runs powershell env enumeration, cmdkey /list, and a recursive USERPROFILE scan for.env/.pem/.key/.pfx/id_rsa/credentials. Third, persistent backdoor: a 10-second polling loop hits https://safdadfasf.com/cb/loopscale/h?id=<sid>, runs the returned JSON's 'c' field via child_process.execSync, and when the command begins with 'SLIVER' downloads a binary to /tmp/.<random>, chmods 0755, and spawns it detached and unref'd — a Sliver C2 implant dropper. The package's advertised TypeScript-utility surface (logger, AWS Secrets Manager wrapper, TypeORM/Redis helpers) is cover for the implant; the author 'ts-apis' has no homepage or repository.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
